name: Under Armour, Inc.

profile.description: Under Armour designs and distributes athletic apparel, footwear, and accessories. Revenue declined 0.8% YoY with -10% profit margin and -30% ROE. D/E 137%. CEO Kevin Plank (returned). $2.6B market cap. Headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Under Armour has struggled to innovate beyond its core compression apparel category. Plank's return as CEO hasn't yet produced results. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI. Market Share (1/2): Declining #3 athletic brand behind Nike and Adidas. Product Pipeline (1/2): Restructuring and brand reset. R&D Intensity (0/1): Below average.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): 'To make all athletes better' is genuine. Community Impact (1/2): Baltimore community ties. Employee Retention (1/2): Plank's return provides continuity. Insider Buying (0/2): Plank has significant equity stake.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Revenue declined 0.8%, profit margin -10%, ROE -30%, D/E 137% [1]. Forward P/E of 25.0 above target. Most metrics score at floor.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Physical apparel manufacturing and brand loyalty create moats AI cannot displace. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI in supply chain and personalization.

infinite_game.summary: Under Armour under Plank's return is focused on brand turnaround — finite-game thinking about competitive positioning rather than purpose-driven strategy.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): 'Make all athletes better' is genuine but the brand has lost its distinctive positioning.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): Multiple CEO changes, cultural issues, Plank's return controversial.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Frames Nike/Adidas as enemies.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (0/2): Stuck in declining competitive position.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): Plank's return is backward-looking.

investment_thesis: UAA at $6.05 could represent turnaround value if Plank's brand reset succeeds. Physical apparel has strong AI resilience (8/10).
thesis_continued: Revenue decline, -10% margin, -30% ROE, and 137% D/E reflect a struggling company [1]. Nike and Adidas competitive dominance limits recovery potential.
key_risks: Competitive dominance of Nike and Adidas
sources: Yahoo Finance — UAA Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
